config_template.input_tensor_b: tensor of shape {N, C, H, W} or scalar
config_template.input_tensor_c: tensor of shape {N, C, H, W} or scalar

applicability_notes: Fuses an elementwise multiply followed by an elementwise add (a*b + c) into a single ttnn.mac kernel launch. Replaces the contiguous chain torch.mul -> torch.add when the add's second operand is independent of the mul output. Verified against golden _golden_function_mac.; constraints: {'scalar_overload': 'input_tensor_b and input_tensor_c may both be python floats (ttnn.mac(t, value1, value2))', 'broadcasting': 'tensor variant tested with all three inputs of identical shape', 'layout': 'inputs must be TILE_LAYOUT on device'}

placement_observations.source: unit_test (ttnn.from_torch(..., layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, device=device) with default memory_config -> DRAM interleaved)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: same shape as input_tensor_a, or scalar immediates (1.0, 5.0, 10.0)
placement_observations.source: unit_test (scalar overload ttnn.mac(input_tensor1, value1, value2) requires no device placement for b/c)
unit_test (output converted via to_layout(ROW_MAJOR) then from_device; no explicit output memory_config given)
